rodin/danaid.note: Corrected 2026-06-30 from legacy y:1.1:180, which presented the recumbent figure on its cut side. Use the scan's Z-up basis and flip around Y so the figure rests on a horizontal base while remaining front-readable.

porcellino.note: Sit the seated boar level on its plinth (raw scan rests ~48deg off-axis); rotation from stable-pose analysis.

greek/kneeling-archer-figure-xii-aphaia-.note: Stand the crouching archer upright on its plinth (raw scan was inverted, plinth on top).

rodin/heroic-head-of-pierre-de-wissant.note: Flip 180deg so the mount stump is the base and the face points to the camera (raw scan was upside-down).

americas/bisj-ancestor-pole-jiem.note: Stand the bisj pole vertical to match its upright siblings (raw scan leaned ~6deg).

michelangelo/dawn.note: Mesh bundles an oversized plain base block larger than the figure; no camera/orientation fixes it. Needs a mesh trim or cleaner re-fetch. Reverted to prior view meanwhile.

michelangelo/madonna-of-the-stairs.note: Keep the face-on relief view, then add a small +10deg roll correction so the slab reads upright instead of diagonally tilted in the live viewer.

roman/portrait-of-julia-msr.note: Use the scan's Z-up axis; stable-pose auto-orientation placed the portrait head on its side.

roman/jupiter-with-thunderbolt-msr.note: Keep the source mesh's Y-up orientation; stable-pose auto-orientation laid the figurine down.

roman/satyr-mask-ra-36d-msr.note: Use the scan's Z-up axis; stable-pose auto-orientation presented the rear/side of the mask.

roman/portrait-of-an-unknown-woman-msr.note: Use the scan's Z-up axis; stable-pose auto-orientation placed the portrait head on its side.

asia/cosmic-buddha.note: Open on the carved front face of the relief; source GLB is already Y-up.

sub-saharan-africa/sapi-portuguese-hunti.note: Roll the oblong horn to a diagonal and tighten the frame so it fills the portrait thumbnail (2.5x coverage) instead of floating in a thin horizontal band.

dying-gaul.note: Base-down + eye-level broadside (coverage-derived) so the dying warrior reads in profile instead of top-down.

asia/brazier-of-rasulid-sultan-met.note: Corrected 2026-06-30: reject the stable-pose rotation, which laid the brazier on its side in the live viewer. The source Met/VNTANA GLB is already Y-up and renders with the four legs down.

greek/athena-parthenos-fragment-smk-cast.note: Manual review: stand the helmet/head fragment upright and face the sculptural side; the stable-pose solve selected the casting support axis.

greek/seated-herakles-aphaia-smk-cast.note: Manual review: keep the stable-pose upright transform, but start from the opposite side so the archer's front and bow read in the gallery thumbnail.

greek/head-of-a-giant-smk-cast.note: Manual review: keep the source orientation so the Pergamon head faces the camera; the stable-pose solve laid the casting plug horizontally.

greek/athena-hope-head-smk-cast.note: Manual review: keep the source orientation so the Athena head opens to the camera; automated long-axis alignment emphasized the support instead of the face.

roman/laurel-wreathed-sacrificial-attend.note: Manual review: the shallow relief renders upright and front-facing in its source orientation.

americas/engraved-bamboo-container-kare-.note: Elongated (aspect 8.9); PCA long-axis aligned to vertical.

americas/stirrup-spout-bottle-feline-and.note: Manual review: use the stable-pose proposal; this VNTANA GLB is componentized but visually coherent.

asia/molded-plaque-couple-met.note: Corrected 2026-06-30: preserve the source Y-up/front-facing orientation. The previous stable-pose rotation opened on the undecorated back and left the plaque diagonal.

sub-saharan-africa/zoomorphic-headrest-n.note: Corrected 2026-06-30: preserve the Met/VNTANA source Y-up pose so the top plank is horizontal and the supports sit below it. Stable-pose rotations made the headrest look fallen over.

americas/kero-beaker-tiwanaku-met.note: Manual review: preserve the source orientation so the kero stands upright instead of following the stable-pose side-rest.

americas/double-bowl-inca-met.note: Corrected 2026-06-30: reject the stable-pose rotation, which floated the double vessel diagonally in the live viewer. Preserve the source/Z-up presentation so the spouts and decorated body read upright.

asia/inscribed-pen-box-met.note: Manual review: preserve the source orientation so the long pen box reads in its catalogued proportions; start the camera from above the decorated face.

asia/tombstone-architectural-niche-met.note: Manual review: preserve the source orientation for the shallow carved slab and open the camera on the relief face.

asia/water-pipe-base-huqqa-poetry-flower.note: Manual review: start from the stable-pose proposal so the huqqa base sits upright.

americas/tuber-inspired-stirrup-spout-bo.note: Manual review: preserve the Met/VNTANA source pose rather than the stable-pose side-rest proposal.

americas/stirrup-spout-bottle-combat-sce.note: Manual review: preserve the Met/VNTANA source pose for this low vessel; the stable-pose proposal rotates the form away from its catalogued stance.

americas/stirrup-spout-bottle-decapitati.note: Manual review: preserve the Met/VNTANA source pose rather than the componentized stable-pose proposal.

americas/old-arrow-maker-edmonia-lewis-s.note: Manual review: preserve the Smithsonian 3D GLB source pose.

americas/helen-ruthven-waterston-edmonia.note: Manual review: preserve the Smithsonian 3D GLB source pose.

renaissance/mater-dolorosa-smk-cast.note: Corrected 2026-07-01: reject the low-confidence stable-pose solve, which showed the hollow underside of the drapery. Source STL is Z-up; a plain -90deg X correction (no yaw) stands the hooded, seated mourning figure upright with clasped hands and base visible.

asia/buddha-of-medicine-bhaishajyaguru-m.note: 2026-07-01: this wide wall-mural scan reads tiny at the default angled 3/4 view/padding, wasting most of the portrait frame. Near-frontal viewDirection + tightened fit (near the framing-utility's padding floor) makes the mural legible and fills the thumbnail; the mesh's own bounding box (likely including backing/matrix material beyond the painted face) is the limiting factor on going tighter.

sub-saharan-africa/ram-mask-bolo-clevela.note: Use the scan source Z-up basis so the ram mask sits upright in the viewer; yaw set to 180deg so the default camera opens on the carved face rather than the hollow back.

sub-saharan-africa/kongo-ntadi-figure-mi.note: Keep the source Y-up orientation; the ntadi figure bounds are already vertically dominant and read upright without additional roll.

sub-saharan-africa/kongo-tusk-relief-nma.note: Museum dimensions identify the long axis as height; the preview GLB arrives X-up, so rotate X to viewer Y to avoid a sideways tusk.

sub-saharan-africa/kongo-tusk-nmafa-96-2.note: Museum dimensions identify the long axis as height; the preview GLB arrives X-up, so rotate X to viewer Y to avoid a sideways tusk.

sub-saharan-africa/temne-mask-mia.note: Accept the auto-orient micro-correction; yaw set to 180deg so the default camera opens on the carved face rather than the back.
